chapter_context.summary: The king's insomnia leads him to read royal chronicles and discover Mordecai's unrewarded deed; Haman enters and is ordered to lead Mordecai through Shushan in royal honor, to his deep humiliation.

outline.text: King's Insomnia; Chronicles Read; Mordecai's Deed Discovered
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King's Insomnia; Chronicles Read; Mordecai's Deed Discovered (6:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Haman Asked How to Honor a Worthy Man; Haman Proposes Royal Procession
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Haman Asked How to Honor a Worthy Man; Haman Proposes Royal Procession (6:4-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: King Commands Haman to Process Mordecai in Royal Honor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Commands Haman to Process Mordecai in Royal Honor (6:10-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

key_themes.title: Unseen Providence
key_themes.description: Although God is not explicitly mentioned by name, His perfect timing, coordination of details, and deliverance are evident throughout the narrative.

key_themes.title: Courage and Duty
key_themes.description: Esther and Mordecai show that standing up for justice and protecting the vulnerable requires courage and taking personal risks.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 6, how can you recognize God's silent directing in your own life's circumstances?
What does it mean for you to act with courage 'for such a time as this' in your sphere of influence?

cultural_historical.title: The Persian Empire and Purim
cultural_historical.text: The events take place in Susa (Shushan), one of the capital cities of the Achaemenid Empire, and explain the origin of the Feast of Purim.
